components.note: first character referring to furniture for storage

柜台.etymology: A compound word combining 柜 (cabinet, cupboard) and 台 (platform, table). The combination refers to a piece of furniture that serves as both storage (cabinet) and work surface (table), typically used for conducting business or providing service.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng dào fúwù guìtái xúnwèn.
examples.en: Please inquire at the service counter.

examples.pinyin: Yínháng guìtái jīntiān hěn máng.
examples.en: The bank counter is very busy today.

滚.etymology: The character combines the water radical 氵 with 衮 as a phonetic component. The water radical suggests flowing, continuous movement, which relates to the rolling motion. The meaning 'get lost' is a colloquial extension of the rolling away motion.

examples.pinyin: Qiú gǔn dào zhuōzi xiàmiàn le.
examples.en: The ball rolled under the table.

examples.pinyin: Xuěqiú yuè gǔn yuè dà.
examples.en: The snowball gets bigger and bigger as it rolls.

锅.etymology: The simplified character combines the metal radical 钅 with 呙 as a phonetic component. The metal radical clearly indicates that this refers to metal cookware. This is a simplification from the traditional form which had a more complex phonetic component.

examples.pinyin: Māma zài guō lǐ zuòfàn.
examples.en: Mom is cooking in the pot.

examples.pinyin: Zhège guō hěn hǎoyòng, bù zhān dǐ.
examples.en: This pot is very useful, it's non-stick.

components.note: first character meaning nation or state
second character meaning border or relationship

国际.etymology: A compound word combining 国 (country, nation) and 际 (boundary, relationship). The meaning is 'between nations' or 'crossing national boundaries', which naturally developed into the modern concept of international. The word emphasizes relationships that span across national borders.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yīgè guójì huìyì.
examples.en: This is an international conference.

examples.zh: 国际贸易对经济很重要。
examples.pinyin: Guójì màoyì duì jīngjì hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: International trade is very important for the economy.

果然.etymology: This compound word combines 果 (fruit, result) with 然 (so, thus). The meaning evolved from 'bearing fruit as expected' to the modern sense of 'indeed, as expected'. Both characters retain their simplified forms unchanged from traditional Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Guǒrán xià yǔ le.
examples.en: It's raining as expected.

examples.zh: 他果然没有来参加会议。
examples.pinyin: Tā guǒrán méiyǒu lái cānjiā huìyì.
examples.en: He indeed didn't come to attend the meeting.

果汁.etymology: A straightforward compound combining 果 (fruit) and 汁 (juice). This modern term emerged as fruit processing became common. Both characters remain unchanged in simplified Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan hē píngguǒ zhī.
examples.en: I like drinking apple juice.

examples.pinyin: Xīnxiān de guǒzhī bǐjiào jiànkāng.
examples.en: Fresh fruit juice is relatively healthy.

components.note: adds sense of stages or distance

过程.etymology: This compound combines 过 (to pass through) with 程 (journey, course). The word conceptualizes a process as a journey with stages to pass through. The simplified 过 replaced traditional 過, removing the 咼 component and simplifying the structure.

examples.pinyin: Xuéxí shì yīgè guòchéng.
examples.en: Learning is a process.

examples.zh: 这个过程需要很长时间才能完成。
examples.pinyin: Zhège guòchéng xūyào hěn cháng shíjiān cái néng wánchéng.
examples.en: This process takes a very long time to complete.

components.note: refers to appropriate measure or limit

过分.etymology: This compound means 'exceeding the proper portion/degree'. 过 (exceed) combined with 分 (portion, measure) creates the sense of going beyond appropriate limits. The simplified 过 replaced traditional 過.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ tài guòfèn le!
examples.en: You've gone too far!

examples.zh: 他认为这个要求过分了。
examples.pinyin: Tā rènwéi zhège yāoqiú guòfèn le.
examples.en: He thinks this request is excessive.

海洋.etymology: This compound combines two water-related characters: 海 (sea) and 洋 (ocean, vast). Together they emphasize the vastness of oceanic waters. Both characters remained unchanged in simplified Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Yú zhù zài hǎiyáng lǐ.
examples.en: Fish live in the ocean.

examples.zh: 海洋覆盖了地球表面的大部分。
examples.pinyin: Hǎiyáng fùgài le dìqiú biǎomiàn de dàbùfèn.
examples.en: The ocean covers most of the Earth's surface.

害羞.etymology: This compound uses 害 in the sense of 'causing a state' combined with 羞 (shame, shyness). The meaning evolved to describe the bashful feeling of social discomfort. Both characters remained unchanged in simplified Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Tā hěn hàixiū, bù gǎn shuōhuà.
examples.en: She's very shy and doesn't dare to speak.

examples.zh: 第一次见面时他显得有些害羞。
examples.pinyin: Dì yī cì jiànmiàn shí tā xiǎnde yǒuxiē hàixiū.
examples.en: He seemed somewhat shy when meeting for the first time.

components.note: refers to time off from work/school

寒假.etymology: This compound combines 寒 (cold, winter) with 假 (vacation). It specifically refers to the winter break in the academic calendar. Both characters remained unchanged in simplified Chinese, with this term being commonly used in educational contexts.

examples.pinyin: Hánjià wǒ yào huí jiā.
examples.en: I want to go home during winter vacation.

examples.zh: 寒假期间学生们可以好好休息。
examples.pinyin: Hánjià qíjiān xuéshengmen kěyǐ hǎohao xiūxi.
examples.en: During winter vacation, students can have a good rest.

汗.etymology: The character combines the water radical 氵 (indicating liquid) with 干 (dry), which provides the phonetic element. The juxtaposition of water and dry creates the meaning of sweat - the liquid that emerges when one is hot or dry.

examples.pinyin: Tā chū le hěn duō hàn.
examples.en: He sweated a lot.

examples.pinyin: Yùndòng hòu mǎn shēn dà hàn.
examples.en: After exercise, he was covered in sweat.

components.note: refers to type of business or trade
refers to work or business field

行业.etymology: This compound word combines 行 (profession, trade) with 业 (occupation, business). Together they form a term referring to specific sectors of economic activity or professional fields.

examples.pinyin: Tā zài IT hángyè gōngzuò.
examples.en: He works in the IT industry.

examples.zh: 每个行业都有自己的特点。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè hángyè dōu yǒu zìjǐ de tèdiǎn.
examples.en: Every industry has its own characteristics.

好处.etymology: This compound combines 好 (good) with 处 (place, point), literally meaning 'good points' or 'good aspects'. It refers to the positive aspects or advantages of something.

examples.pinyin: Yùndòng yǒu hěn duō hǎochu.
examples.en: Exercise has many benefits.

examples.zh: 这个决定对我们有什么好处？
examples.pinyin: Zhège juédìng duì wǒmen yǒu shénme hǎochu?
examples.en: What benefits does this decision have for us?

components.note: used as an adverb meaning 'quite' or 'rather'

好像.etymology: This phrase combines 好 (used here as an intensifying adverb meaning 'quite') with 像 (to resemble, be like). Together they express the idea of something appearing to be similar to or resembling something else.

examples.pinyin: Tā hǎoxiàng hěn lèi.
examples.en: He seems very tired.

examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng hǎoxiàng yào xiàyǔ le.
examples.en: It looks like it's going to rain.

components.note: refers to numerical code or digit

号码.etymology: This compound combines 号 (number, signal) with 码 (code, number). Both characters relate to numerical identification, creating a reinforced meaning of numerical designation or identification number.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ de diànhuà hàomǎ shì duōshao?
examples.en: What's your phone number?

examples.pinyin: Qǐng jìzhù zhège fángjiān hàomǎ.
examples.en: Please remember this room number.

components.note: refers to required standard or specification

合格.etymology: This compound combines 合 (to fit, conform to) with 格 (standard, specification). Together they mean meeting or conforming to required standards or qualifications.

examples.pinyin: Tā de chéngjì hégé le.
examples.en: His grades are passing.

examples.pinyin: Zhège chǎnpǐn zhìliàng bù hégé.
examples.en: This product's quality doesn't meet standards.

components.note: expresses the concept of matching or fitting

合适.etymology: A compound word where both characters contribute to the meaning of suitability. 合 originally depicted a mouth with a lid, suggesting things that fit together well. 适 combines the movement radical 辶 with 舍, originally meaning to arrive at the right place, extending to mean suitable or appropriate.

examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu hěn héshì.
examples.en: This piece of clothing fits very well.

examples.zh: 我觉得现在不是合适的时候。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ juéde xiànzài bù shì héshì de shíhou.
examples.en: I don't think now is the right time.

components.note: indicates harmony and lack of conflict

和平.etymology: This compound combines 和 (harmony, peaceful) with 平 (calm, level, stable). Together they create the concept of peace - a state of harmony and calm without conflict or disturbance.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen dōu xīwàng shìjiè hépíng.
examples.en: We all hope for world peace.

examples.zh: 和平解决争端比战争更好。
examples.pinyin: Hépíng jiějué zhēngduān bǐ zhànzhēng gèng hǎo.
examples.en: Peaceful resolution of disputes is better than war.

盒子.etymology: A compound word consisting of 盒 (box) and the diminutive suffix 子. The character 盒 combines 合 (to join, fit together) with 皿 (vessel), suggesting a container that closes or fits together. This reflects the functional nature of boxes as containers with fitting lids.

examples.pinyin: Zhège hézi hěn piàoliang.
examples.en: This box is very beautiful.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ wénjiàn fàng zài hézi lǐ.
examples.en: Please put the documents in the box.

后悔.etymology: A compound word expressing retrospective remorse. 后 (after) indicates the temporal aspect of looking back, while 悔 contains the heart radical 忄, indicating an emotional state. The combination suggests the feeling of wishing one had acted differently after the fact.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn hòuhuǐ méiyou xuéxí.
examples.en: I really regret not studying.

examples.zh: 做决定前要想清楚，免得后悔。
examples.pinyin: Zuò juédìng qián yào xiǎng qīngchu, miǎnde hòuhuǐ.
examples.en: Think clearly before making decisions, so you won't regret it.

厚.etymology: The character combines 厂 (cliff) with internal components including 日 and 子. The original meaning related to the substantial nature of thick cliffs or substantial shelter. Over time, it developed the general meaning of thickness in physical dimensions and metaphorically generous or substantial character.

examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū hěn hòu.
examples.en: This book is very thick.

examples.zh: 冬天要穿厚一点的衣服。
examples.pinyin: Dōngtiān yào chuān hòu yīdiǎn de yīfu.
examples.en: In winter, you should wear thicker clothes.

互相.etymology: A compound word where both characters emphasize mutual relationship. 互 originally depicted hands working together, suggesting reciprocal action. 相 combines 木 (tree) with 目 (eye), originally meaning to observe or examine, which extended to mean mutual interaction or relationship.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào hùxiāng bāngzhù.
examples.en: We should help each other.

examples.zh: 同事之间应该互相理解和支持。
examples.pinyin: Tóngshì zhījiān yīnggāi hùxiāng lǐjiě hé zhīchí.
examples.en: Colleagues should understand and support each other.

花生.etymology: A compound word literally meaning 'flower growth' or 'flower product.' The name reflects how peanuts develop from the flowers of the peanut plant. Unlike tree nuts, peanuts are legumes that flower above ground but develop their pods underground, making the 'flower' reference particularly apt for this unique growing pattern.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan chī huāshēng.
examples.en: I like eating peanuts.

examples.zh: 这道菜里有花生，过敏的人不能吃。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài lǐ yǒu huāshēng, guòmǐn de rén bù néng chī.
examples.en: This dish contains peanuts, people with allergies cannot eat it.

components.note: contributes to the meaning of划分or cutting through

划.etymology: Originally meant to cut or divide. The character combines 戈 (weapon) with the knife radical 刂, suggesting cutting or划分. The meaning extended to划船 (rowing) through the motion of cutting through water with oars.

examples.pinyin: Huá chuán hěn yǒu qù.
examples.en: Rowing is very interesting.

examples.zh: 他们在湖上划了一个小时。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài hú shàng huále yī gè xiǎoshí.
examples.en: They rowed on the lake for an hour.

components.note: provides phonetic component and suggests smooth surface

滑.etymology: The character combines the water radical 氵 with 骨 (bone). The water element suggests wetness that causes slipperiness, while 骨 provides phonetic value and may suggest the smooth surface of bone.

examples.pinyin: Dìmiàn hěn huá.
examples.en: The ground is very slippery.

examples.pinyin: Xiǎoxīn bié zài bīng shàng huádǎole.
examples.en: Be careful not to slip and fall on the ice.

化学.etymology: A compound word combining 化 (transformation/change) and 学 (study/learning). The term reflects chemistry's focus on studying chemical transformations and reactions. This term was coined in modern Chinese to translate the Western concept of chemistry.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan huàxué kè.
examples.en: I like chemistry class.

examples.zh: 他在大学学习化学工程。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài dàxué xuéxí huàxué gōngchéng.
examples.en: He studies chemical engineering at university.

怀疑.etymology: A compound combining 怀 (to hold in one's mind/heart) and 疑 (to doubt). Together they express holding doubtful thoughts or uncertain feelings about something. Both characters relate to mental and emotional states.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ huáiyí tā shuō de huà.
examples.en: I doubt what he said.

examples.zh: 警察怀疑这个人和案件有关。
examples.pinyin: Jǐngchá huáiyí zhège rén hé ànjiàn yǒuguān.
examples.en: The police suspect this person is related to the case.

components.note: provides structure, possibly representing shelter where fire burns

灰.etymology: The character depicts fire 火 under a shelter or cliff 厂. This represents the concept of ash that remains after fire burns, typically in a sheltered location like a hearth or cave. The gray color association comes from the color of ash.

examples.pinyin: Tā chuānzhe huīsè de kùzi.
examples.en: He is wearing gray pants.

examples.zh: 烧完纸后留下了很多灰。
examples.pinyin: Shāo wán zhǐ hòu liúxiàle hěnduō huī.
examples.en: After burning the paper, a lot of ash was left behind.

恢复.etymology: This compound word combines 恢 (to restore, vast) and 复 (to return, again) to express the concept of restoration or recovery. Both characters reinforce the meaning of returning to a previous state.

examples.pinyin: Tā de shēntǐ zhèngzài huīfù.
examples.en: His health is recovering.

examples.zh: 经济需要时间才能完全恢复。
examples.pinyin: Jīngjì xūyào shíjiān cái néng wánquán huīfù.
examples.en: The economy needs time to fully recover.

婚礼.etymology: This compound combines 婚 (marriage) with the female radical 女, and 礼 (ceremony, ritual) to specifically denote a wedding ceremony or marriage ritual.

examples.pinyin: Tāmen de hūnlǐ hěn piàoliang.
examples.en: Their wedding was very beautiful.

examples.zh: 传统婚礼通常有很多仪式。
examples.pinyin: Chuántǒng hūnlǐ tōngcháng yǒu hěn duō yíshì.
examples.en: Traditional weddings usually have many rituals.

火柴.etymology: This compound literally means 'fire wood', combining 火 (fire) and 柴 (firewood) to refer to matches - small wooden sticks that create fire when struck.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ yǒu huǒchái ma?
examples.en: Do you have matches?

examples.zh: 现在很少人用火柴点烟了。
examples.pinyin: Xiànzài hěn shǎo rén yòng huǒchái diǎnyān le.
examples.en: Few people use matches to light cigarettes nowadays.

积极.etymology: This compound combines 积 (to accumulate) and 极 (extreme, pole) to express the concept of being extremely active or having accumulated positive energy - hence active and positive.

examples.pinyin: Tā duì gōngzuò hěn jījí.
examples.en: He is very active at work.

examples.zh: 我们应该积极面对生活中的困难。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi jījí miànduì shēnghuó zhōng de kùnnan.
examples.en: We should actively face the difficulties in life.

基础.etymology: A compound word combining 基 (foundation, base) and 础 (foundation stone). Both characters relate to the concept of a solid base or groundwork, creating a reinforced meaning of fundamental support or basis.

examples.pinyin: Xué hǎo Hànyǔ jīchǔ hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: Learning Chinese basics well is very important.

examples.zh: 这个项目需要坚实的基础。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù xūyào jiānshí de jīchǔ.
examples.en: This project needs a solid foundation.

components.note: first character meaning to stimulate or arouse
second character meaning movement or motion

激动.etymology: A compound word combining 激 (to stimulate, arouse) and 动 (to move, motion). The combination suggests emotional stirring or being moved to action, creating the meaning of excitement or being excited.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn jīdòng!
examples.en: I'm very excited!

examples.zh: 听到这个好消息，大家都很激动。
examples.pinyin: Tīngdào zhège hǎo xiāoxi, dàjiā dōu hěn jīdòng.
examples.en: Everyone was very excited to hear this good news.

components.note: first character meaning to reach or catch up with

及时.etymology: A compound word combining 及 (to reach, arrive at) and 时 (time). The meaning relates to reaching or catching up with the right time, hence being timely or punctual.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng jíshí huífù.
examples.en: Please reply in time.

examples.zh: 及时的帮助让我们避免了麻烦。
examples.pinyin: Jíshí de bāngzhù ràng wǒmen bìmiǎnle máfan.
examples.en: Timely help helped us avoid trouble.

components.note: first character meaning immediately or namely
second character meaning to make or cause

即使.etymology: A compound word combining 即 (immediately, namely) and 使 (to make, cause). Together they form a conjunction expressing a hypothetical condition that doesn't change the result, meaning 'even if' or 'even though'.

examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ xià yǔ, wǒ yě yào qù.
examples.en: Even if it rains, I still want to go.

examples.zh: 即使困难很多，我们也不会放弃。
examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ kùnnan hěn duō, wǒmen yě bù huì fàngqì.
examples.en: Even though there are many difficulties, we won't give up.

components.note: first character meaning already or since
second character meaning so or in that way

既然.etymology: A compound word combining 既 (already, since) and 然 (so, thus). Together they form a conjunction indicating acceptance of a fact and drawing a logical conclusion, meaning 'since' or 'now that'.

examples.pinyin: Jìrán láile, jiù hǎohǎo xuéxí.
examples.en: Since you're here, study well.

examples.zh: 既然你不喜欢，我们就换一个地方。
examples.pinyin: Jìrán nǐ bù xǐhuan, wǒmen jiù huàn yīgè dìfang.
examples.en: Since you don't like it, let's change to another place.

components.note: first character meaning to continue or inherit
second character meaning to continue or renew

继续.etymology: A compound word combining 继 (to continue, succeed) and 续 (to continue, extend). Both characters carry the meaning of continuation, creating a reinforced sense of ongoing action or maintaining something without interruption.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jìxù gōngzuò ba.
examples.en: Let's continue working.

examples.zh: 尽管遇到困难，他还是继续努力。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn yùdào kùnnan, tā háishi jìxù nǔlì.
examples.en: Despite encountering difficulties, he continued to work hard.

components.note: 宀 radical indicating building or shelter

寄.etymology: A phonosemantic character with 宀 (roof) as the semantic component suggesting shelter or temporary lodging, and 奇 providing the sound. The meaning evolved from 'to lodge temporarily' to 'to entrust' and then 'to send by mail'.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào jì yī fēng xìn.
examples.en: I want to mail a letter.

examples.zh: 请把这个包裹寄到北京。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ zhège bāoguǒ jì dào Běijīng.
examples.en: Please send this package to Beijing.

components.note: first character meaning to add or increase
second character meaning work shift or class

加班.etymology: A compound word combining 加 (to add, increase) and 班 (shift, class). The meaning refers to adding extra work shifts or extending work hours beyond the normal schedule, hence working overtime.

examples.pinyin: Jīntiān wǒ yào jiābān.
examples.en: I have to work overtime today.

examples.pinyin: Jīngcháng jiābān duì shēntǐ bù hǎo.
examples.en: Working overtime frequently is bad for your health.

家具.etymology: A compound word combining 家 (home/family) and 具 (tools/implements). The term literally means 'household implements' and refers to movable objects that make a home functional and comfortable.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào mǎi xīn jiājù.
examples.en: We need to buy new furniture.

examples.zh: 这套家具很贵但是质量很好。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tào jiājù hěn guì dànshì zhìliàng hěn hǎo.
examples.en: This set of furniture is expensive but the quality is very good.

假.etymology: The character combines the person radical 亻 with the phonetic component 叚. Originally meant 'to borrow' but extended to mean 'fake' or 'false' as borrowed things are temporary substitutes. It also means 'vacation' in modern usage.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì jiǎ qián.
examples.en: This is fake money.

examples.zh: 他说的话是假的，不要相信。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuō de huà shì jiǎ de, bùyào xiāngxìn.
examples.en: What he said is false, don't believe it.

价格.etymology: A compound word where 价 (simplified from 價, meaning price/value) combines with 格 (standard/pattern). Together they form the modern term for 'price' as a standard measure of value in commerce.

examples.pinyin: Zhège jiàgé tài gāo le.
examples.en: This price is too high.

examples.zh: 油价格最近一直在上涨。
examples.pinyin: Yóu jiàgé zuìjìn yīzhí zài shàngzhǎng.
examples.en: Oil prices have been rising recently.

坚持.etymology: A compound word combining 坚 (firm, simplified from 堅) and 持 (to hold/maintain). The traditional 堅 was composed of multiple earth radicals indicating solidity, while simplified 坚 retains the core meaning of firmness.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào jiānchí xuéxí zhōngwén.
examples.en: I want to persist in learning Chinese.

examples.zh: 虽然很困难，但他坚持完成了任务。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán hěn kùnnán, dàn tā jiānchí wánchéng le rènwù.
examples.en: Although it was difficult, he persisted and completed the task.

减少.etymology: A compound word combining 减 (to reduce, simplified from 減) and 少 (few/little). The traditional 減 contained the water radical indicating flow or change, while simplified 减 uses the ice radical 冫 to maintain the semantic connection.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào jiǎnshǎo chī táng.
examples.en: I want to reduce eating sugar.

examples.zh: 公司决定减少开支来节省成本。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī juédìng jiǎnshǎo kāizhī lái jiéshěng chéngběn.
examples.en: The company decided to reduce expenses to save costs.

components.note: simplified from 議, indicates verbal proposal

建议.etymology: A compound word combining 建 (to build/establish) and 议 (to discuss, simplified from 議). Together they mean to 'build up' or 'establish' a proposal or discussion point, hence 'suggest' or 'suggestion'.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì nǐ zǎodiǎn shuìjiào.
examples.en: I suggest you go to bed early.

examples.zh: 老师接受了学生们的建议，改变了考试时间。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī jiēshòu le xuéshēngmen de jiànyì, gǎibiàn le kǎoshì shíjiān.
examples.en: The teacher accepted the students' suggestions and changed the exam time.

交流.etymology: This compound word combines 交 (to exchange, hand over) with 流 (to flow). Together they create the concept of flowing exchange or communication between parties, emphasizing the dynamic and reciprocal nature of information or cultural exchange.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào duō jiāoliú.
examples.en: We need to communicate more.

examples.pinyin: Guójì wénhuà jiāoliú hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: International cultural exchange is very important.

交通.etymology: This compound combines 交 (to intersect, cross) with 通 (to pass through, connect). The combination originally described the crossing and connecting of roads and pathways, which evolved into the modern meaning of traffic and transportation systems.

examples.pinyin: Jiāotōng hěn yōngjǐ.
examples.en: The traffic is very congested.

examples.zh: 这个城市的交通系统很发达。
examples.pinyin: Zhège chéngshì de jiāotōng xìtǒng hěn fādá.
examples.en: This city's transportation system is very developed.

脚.etymology: The character combines the flesh radical 月 indicating a body part with the phonetic component 却. This clearly categorizes it as referring to a part of the human body, specifically the foot, which is the lowest part used for walking and standing.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ de jiǎo hěn téng.
examples.en: My foot hurts a lot.

examples.pinyin: Tā guāng zhe jiǎo zài shātān shàng zǒu.
examples.en: He walked barefoot on the beach.

接受.etymology: This compound combines 接 (to receive, connect with) and 受 (to receive, bear, endure). Both characters involve the concept of receiving, and together they emphasize the willing reception or acceptance of something offered, whether ideas, gifts, or situations.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiēshòu nǐ de jiànyì.
examples.en: I accept your suggestion.

examples.zh: 他很难接受这个坏消息。
examples.pinyin: Tā hěn nán jiēshòu zhège huài xiāoxi.
examples.en: He finds it hard to accept this bad news.

节约.etymology: This compound combines 节 (to control, moderate) with 约 (to bind, restrict, economize). The traditional 節 was simplified to 节. Together they emphasize the concept of controlled restriction or moderation, particularly in the use of resources, leading to the meaning of saving or being economical.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào jiēyuē yòng shuǐ.
examples.en: We should save water.

examples.zh: 节约能源对环境很重要。
examples.pinyin: Jiēyuē néngyuán duì huánjìng hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: Saving energy is very important for the environment.

components.note: provides the sense of completion or conclusion
provides the sense of outcome or consequence

结果.etymology: A compound word combining 结 (to tie, conclude) and 果 (fruit, result). The word metaphorically represents the 'fruit' or outcome that is 'tied' to or results from a particular action or process. This usage reflects the Chinese conceptual linking of results with the ripening of fruit.

examples.pinyin: Kǎoshì de jiēguǒ hěn hǎo.
examples.en: The exam results are very good.

examples.zh: 这个决定的结果让大家都很满意。
examples.pinyin: Zhège juédìng de jiēguǒ ràng dàjiā dōu hěn mǎnyì.
examples.en: Everyone is satisfied with the result of this decision.

components.note: provides the sense of clarifying or making clear
adds the sense of setting free understanding or making things clear

解释.etymology: A compound word combining 解 (to untie, solve) and 释 (to release, explain). The word literally means 'to untie and release' understanding, representing the process of clarifying confusion and setting knowledge free. Both characters contribute to the meaning of making something clear and understandable.

examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī jiěshì le zhège wèntí.
examples.en: The teacher explained this problem.

examples.zh: 他详细解释了自己迟到的原因。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiángxì jiěshì le zìjǐ chídào de yuányīn.
examples.en: He explained in detail the reason for being late.

components.note: provides phonetic value and suggests temporary nature

借.etymology: The character consists of the person radical 亻 and 昔 (former times). The phonetic component 昔 suggests something temporary or from the past, fitting the concept of borrowing as a temporary transfer. The character represents the human action of temporarily transferring possession of something with the expectation of return.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng jiè nǐ de shū.
examples.en: I want to borrow your book.

examples.zh: 银行可以借钱给需要的人。
examples.pinyin: Yínháng kěyǐ jiè qián gěi xūyào de rén.
examples.en: Banks can lend money to people who need it.

components.note: top part represents human connection to precious metal
middle part suggests royalty and value
bottom dots represent small pieces of gold

金.etymology: The character depicts gold ore or nuggets. The traditional form showed a more explicit representation of gold pieces in the earth. As the radical for metals, it forms the basis for many characters related to metals and metallic objects. The simplified form maintains the essential structure while streamlining the strokes.

examples.pinyin: Zhège jièzhi shì jīn de.
examples.en: This ring is made of gold.

examples.pinyin: Jīnjià zuìjìn shàngzhǎng le hěn duō.
examples.en: The price of gold has risen a lot recently.

components.note: provides the sense of tightness or pressure
adds the sense of being stretched or under strain

紧张.etymology: A compound word combining 紧 (tight, urgent) and 张 (to stretch, open). The combination creates a sense of being both tight and stretched, perfectly capturing the physical and emotional state of tension. The word reflects the dual nature of nervousness as both constricting and stretching one's comfort zone.

examples.pinyin: Kǎoshì qián wǒ hěn jǐnzhāng.
examples.en: I'm very nervous before the exam.

examples.zh: 工作压力让他感到非常紧张。
examples.pinyin: Gōngzuò yālì ràng tā gǎndào fēicháng jǐnzhāng.
examples.en: Work pressure makes him feel very tense.

components.note: adds the sense of oversight or consideration

尽管.etymology: A compound word combining 尽 (to exhaust, completely) and 管 (to manage, control). The phrase literally means 'completely manage' or 'thoroughly consider,' which evolved to mean 'despite fully considering' or 'although completely acknowledging.' This reflects the logical progression from complete consideration to concessive meaning.

examples.zh: 尽管很累，他还是坚持工作。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn hěn lèi, tā háishì jiānchí gōngzuò.
examples.en: Although he's very tired, he still persists in working.

examples.zh: 尽管天气不好，比赛还是如期进行了。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn tiānqì bù hǎo, bǐsài háishì rúqī jìnxíng le.
examples.en: Despite the bad weather, the competition proceeded as scheduled.

components.note: second character expressing action or performance

进行.etymology: This compound verb combines 进 (to advance, enter) with 行 (to go, perform). The pairing emphasizes both the initiation and execution of an action. It developed as a formal way to express conducting or carrying out activities, particularly in official or business contexts.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào jìnxíng tǎolùn.
examples.en: We need to have a discussion.

examples.pinyin: Zhè xiàng gōngzuò zhèngzài jìnxíng zhōng.
examples.en: This work is currently in progress.

components.note: first character relating to management or governing principles
second character relating to assistance or relief

经济.etymology: Originally meaning 'managing and helping the people' (经世济民), this term evolved to refer to economic systems. The 经 component suggests systematic management, while 济 implies providing assistance or relief. Together they form the modern concept of economy as a system for managing resources to benefit society.

examples.pinyin: Zhōngguó jīngjì fāzhǎn hěn kuài.
examples.en: China's economy is developing very quickly.

examples.pinyin: Tā xuéxí jīngjìxué zhuānyè.
examples.en: He studies economics as his major.

components.note: second character reinforcing the management concept

经理.etymology: This compound combines 经 (to manage, handle affairs) with 理 (to organize, manage). Both characters relate to management and organization, creating a term that emphasizes the comprehensive nature of managerial responsibilities. It became the standard term for business managers in modern Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì wǒmen gōngsī de jīnglǐ.
examples.en: He is the manager of our company.

examples.zh: 经理正在开会讨论新项目。
examples.pinyin: Jīnglǐ zhèngzài kāihuì tǎolùn xīn xiàngmù.
examples.en: The manager is in a meeting discussing the new project.

components.note: first character expressing the process of going through something
second character relating to testing or proving through practice

经验.etymology: This compound combines 经 (to go through, experience) with 验 (to test, verify). The pairing suggests knowledge gained through going through situations and having them tested or proven in practice. It emphasizes that true experience comes from both encountering situations and learning from their outcomes.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ méiyǒu gōngzuò jīngyàn.
examples.en: I don't have work experience.

examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu fēngfù de jiàoxué jīngyàn.
examples.en: He has rich teaching experience.

components.note: first character expressing the refined or essential nature
second character relating to spiritual or mental aspects

精神.etymology: This compound pairs 精 (essence, refined) with 神 (spirit, divine power). Together they refer to the essential spiritual or mental aspect of a person. The combination emphasizes both the refined nature and the intangible, spiritual quality of mental states and attitudes.

examples.pinyin: Tā jīngshén hěn hǎo.
examples.en: He is in good spirits.

examples.zh: 我们要学习他的团队精神。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào xuéxí tā de tuánduì jīngshén.
examples.en: We should learn from his team spirit.

components.note: second character expressing the final or actual result

究竟.etymology: This compound combines 究 (to investigate thoroughly) with 竟 (actually, in the end). The pairing suggests getting to the bottom of something through investigation to find what is actually the case. It evolved to express emphasis in questions or statements about what is really true.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ jiūjìng xiǎng yào shénme?
examples.en: What exactly do you want?

examples.pinyin: Zhè jiūjìng shì zěnme huí shì?
examples.en: What exactly is going on here?

components.note: provides pronunciation, composed of two 火 characters stacked

谈.etymology: This character uses the speech radical 讠 to indicate verbal communication. The phonetic component 炎 (flame) provides the pronunciation. The traditional form was 談, but it was simplified by replacing the complex right side with 炎. The character emphasizes conversational speech and discussion.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen lái tántan zhège wèntí.
examples.en: Let's talk about this problem.

examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhèngzài tán shēngyì.
examples.en: They are discussing business.

components.note: provides pronunciation and suggests something refined or processed

糖.etymology: This character combines the rice radical 米 with 唐 as a phonetic component. The rice radical indicates its origin from processed grains, while 唐 provides the pronunciation. Historically, sugar was often made from rice or other grains in China, making the rice radical semantically appropriate for this sweet substance.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuān chī táng.
examples.en: I don't like to eat candy.

examples.zh: 医生说他的血糖太高了。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō tā de xuètáng tài gāo le.
examples.en: The doctor said his blood sugar is too high.

躺.etymology: This character combines 身 (body) with 尚 (still/yet) as a phonetic component. The semantic element 身 clearly indicates this verb relates to bodily position, specifically the horizontal resting position of lying down.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng tǎng yīhuìr.
examples.en: I want to lie down for a while.

examples.pinyin: Tā tǎng zài shāfā shàng kàn diànshì.
examples.en: He is lying on the sofa watching TV.

趟.etymology: This character combines the semantic radical 走 (to walk/go) with the phonetic component 尚. The walking radical emphasizes the concept of travel or movement, making it suitable for counting trips or journeys.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ qù le yī tàng shāngdiàn.
examples.en: I made a trip to the store.

examples.pinyin: Zhè tàng huǒchē hěn kuài.
examples.en: This train is very fast.

components.note: provides sound tàng and reinforces hot liquid concept

烫.etymology: This simplified character combines 火 (fire) with 汤 (soup/hot water) as both phonetic and semantic component. The combination emphasizes extreme heat, particularly the dangerous heat that can burn or scald.

examples.pinyin: Shuǐ hěn tàng, xiǎoxīn!
examples.en: The water is very hot, be careful!

examples.pinyin: Tā bèi tàngshāng le shǒu.
examples.en: He scalded his hand.

逃.etymology: This character uses the walking radical 辶 to indicate movement, combined with 兆 as a phonetic element. The walking radical emphasizes the physical act of fleeing or running away from danger or unwanted situations.

examples.pinyin: Xiǎotōu táopǎo le.
examples.en: The thief ran away.

examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎng táobì zérèn.
examples.en: He wants to escape responsibility.

components.note: indicates this is a tree fruit

桃.etymology: This character combines the wood radical 木 with the phonetic component 兆. The wood radical clearly indicates this refers to a tree fruit, while 兆 provides the pronunciation táo for this popular stone fruit.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān chī táozi.
examples.en: I like to eat peaches.

examples.pinyin: Táohuā hěn piàoliang.
examples.en: Peach blossoms are very beautiful.

components.note: seeking or requesting information or opinions

讨论.etymology: This compound word combines 讨 (to seek/ask for) and 论 (to discuss/argue). Together they create the meaning of actively seeking different viewpoints and exchanging ideas through systematic discussion. Both characters contain the speech radical 讠, emphasizing verbal communication.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen tǎolùn zhège wèntí.
examples.en: We discuss this problem.

examples.pinyin: Jīngguò tǎolùn, dàjiā tóngyì le.
examples.en: After discussion, everyone agreed.

components.note: here meaning to invite or provoke

讨厌.etymology: This compound combines 讨 (to seek/provoke) with 厌 (to dislike/detest). The meaning evolved to describe something that provokes dislike or negative feelings. It can function as both a verb (to dislike) and an adjective (annoying/disgusting).

examples.pinyin: Wǒ tǎoyàn xiàyǔ tiān.
examples.en: I dislike rainy days.

examples.pinyin: Zhège rén zhēn tǎoyàn!
examples.en: This person is really annoying!

套.etymology: This character originally depicted something that covers or encompasses completely. The outer structure suggests the idea of enclosing or containing multiple items, leading to its use for sets, suits, or as a classifier for grouped items.

examples.pinyin: yī tào yīfu
examples.en: a set of clothes

examples.pinyin: Tā mǎi le yī tào fángzi.
examples.en: He bought an apartment.

components.note: refers to specific aspects or elements

特点.etymology: A compound word combining 特 (special) and 点 (point). The word literally means 'special points' referring to distinctive aspects or features that make something unique or identifiable.

examples.pinyin: zhè ge chéng shì yǒu shén me tè diǎn?
examples.en: What are the characteristics of this city?

examples.zh: 中国菜的特点是味道丰富。
examples.pinyin: zhōng guó cài de tè diǎn shì wèi dào fēng fù.
examples.en: The characteristic of Chinese cuisine is its rich flavors.

特殊.etymology: A compound word combining 特 (special) and 殊 (different, outstanding). Both characters reinforce the meaning of being unusual or exceptional, creating an emphatic expression for something that stands out from the ordinary.

examples.pinyin: jīn tiān shì tè shū de rì zi.
examples.en: Today is a special day.

examples.zh: 这种情况需要特殊处理。
examples.pinyin: zhè zhǒng qíng kuàng xū yào tè shū chǔ lǐ.
examples.en: This situation requires special handling.

components.note: radical indicating illness or physical discomfort

疼.etymology: The character combines the sickness radical 疒 with 冬 (winter) as a phonetic component. The semantic component indicates physical discomfort or pain, while the phonetic component originally provided pronunciation guidance.

examples.pinyin: wǒ tóu téng.
examples.en: I have a headache.

examples.pinyin: tā de tuǐ hái téng bu téng?
examples.en: Does her leg still hurt?

疼爱.english: to love dearly / to dote on
疼爱.etymology: A compound word combining 疼 (to ache, to feel pain) and 爱 (to love). The word metaphorically suggests loving someone so much that their pain becomes your own pain, indicating deep affection and protective care.

examples.pinyin: yé ye hěn téng ài sūn zi.
examples.en: Grandpa loves his grandson dearly.

examples.pinyin: fù mǔ téng ài hái zi shì tiān xìng.
examples.en: It's natural for parents to dote on their children.

踢.etymology: The character combines the foot radical 足 with 易 as a phonetic component. The semantic component clearly indicates an action performed with the foot, while the phonetic component provides pronunciation guidance.

examples.pinyin: tā tī zú qiú.
examples.en: He kicks the soccer ball.

examples.pinyin: xiǎo xīn bié tī dào zhuō zi tuǐ.
examples.en: Be careful not to kick the table leg.

提前.etymology: A compound word combining 提 (to lift, to raise) and 前 (front, before). The combination literally means 'to lift forward' or 'to move ahead', referring to moving something to an earlier time than originally planned.

examples.pinyin: huì yì tí qián le.
examples.en: The meeting has been moved up.

examples.pinyin: qǐng tí qián sān tiān yù dìng jiǔ diàn.
examples.en: Please book the hotel three days in advance.

components.note: relates to physical or experiential aspect
relates to testing or experiencing something

体验.etymology: A compound word combining 体 (body, form) and 验 (to examine, test). The word emphasizes gaining knowledge through direct personal involvement rather than theoretical understanding. Both characters were simplified from their traditional forms 體 and 驗.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng tǐyàn Zhōngguó wénhuà.
examples.en: I want to experience Chinese culture.

examples.zh: 这次旅行给了我很好的体验。
examples.pinyin: Zhè cì lǚxíng gěile wǒ hěn hǎo de tǐyàn.
examples.en: This trip gave me a very good experience.

components.note: refers to the empty space above

天空.etymology: A compound word combining 天 (heaven, sky) and 空 (empty, void). The word literally means 'heavenly void' or 'empty heaven', referring to the vast space above the earth. This combination emphasizes both the celestial nature and the openness of the sky.

examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng hěn lán.
examples.en: The sky is very blue.

examples.zh: 晚上的天空有很多星星。
examples.pinyin: Wǎnshang de tiānkōng yǒu hěn duō xīngxing.
examples.en: There are many stars in the night sky.

添加.etymology: A compound word combining two characters with similar meanings: 添 (to add, increase) and 加 (to add, plus). This redundant construction strengthens the meaning of addition or supplementation. The combination is commonly used in formal or technical contexts.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng tiānjiā nǐ de míngzi.
examples.en: Please add your name.

examples.zh: 我们需要添加更多的信息。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào tiānjiā gèng duō de xìnxī.
examples.en: We need to add more information.

田.etymology: Originally a pictograph depicting agricultural fields divided by pathways or irrigation channels. The character shows a square divided into four sections, representing cultivated farmland. This is one of the oldest Chinese characters and serves as a radical for many agriculture-related characters.

examples.pinyin: Nóngmín zài tián lǐ gōngzuò.
examples.en: Farmers work in the fields.

examples.pinyin: Zhè piàn tián zhòngle hěn duō shuǐdào.
examples.en: This field has a lot of rice planted in it.

甜.etymology: The character combines 甘 (sweet) and 舌 (tongue) to represent the sensation of sweetness as perceived by the tongue. This logical construction directly relates to the physical experience of tasting something sweet. The character has remained relatively unchanged from ancient forms.

examples.pinyin: Zhège píngguǒ hěn tián.
examples.en: This apple is very sweet.

examples.zh: 小孩子都喜欢吃甜的东西。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo háizi dōu xǐhuan chī tián de dōngxi.
examples.en: Children all like to eat sweet things.

components.note: means to fill or stuff something
refers to empty spaces or blanks

填空.etymology: A compound word combining 填 (to fill) and 空 (empty, blank). The term literally means 'fill empty spaces' and is commonly used in educational contexts for exercises where students fill in missing words or information in sentences or texts.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng tiánkōng zhège jùzi.
examples.en: Please fill in the blanks in this sentence.

examples.zh: 填空题比选择题难一些。
examples.pinyin: Tiánkōng tí bǐ xuǎnzé tí nán yīxiē.
examples.en: Fill-in-the-blank questions are a bit harder than multiple choice questions.

调节.etymology: A compound word combining 调 (to adjust, tune) and 节 (to regulate, control). Both characters relate to making adjustments or maintaining balance. The simplified forms come from traditional 調 and 節, with significant structural changes in both characters.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng tiáojié yīnliàng.
examples.en: Please adjust the volume.

examples.zh: 我们需要调节室内温度。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào tiáojié shìnèi wēndù.
examples.en: We need to regulate the indoor temperature.

components.meaning: to pick, to carry, to provoke
components.note: First character meaning to select or provoke
Second character meaning battle or combat

挑战.etymology: This compound word combines 挑 (to pick/provoke) and 战 (battle/war). The meaning evolved from 'provoking a battle' to the modern sense of 'challenge'. Both characters retain their simplified forms in this word.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yí gè hěn dà de tiǎozhàn.
examples.en: This is a big challenge.

examples.zh: 我们要挑战自己的极限。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào tiǎozhàn zìjǐ de jíxiàn.
examples.en: We need to challenge our own limits.

贴.etymology: The character originally meant to subsidize or add money. The traditional form used 貝 (shell) radical relating to money. The meaning extended to 'sticking' things together, possibly from the idea of adding or attaching value.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ zhàopiàn tiē zài qiáng shàng.
examples.en: Please stick the photo on the wall.

examples.zh: 她在日记本上贴了很多贴纸。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài rìjìběn shàng tiēle hěn duō tiēzhǐ.
examples.en: She stuck many stickers in her diary.

铁.etymology: The simplified form uses 钅 (metal radical) and 失 (to lose) for phonetic value. The traditional form 鐵 used the full 金 (metal) radical. The character has consistently meant iron throughout Chinese history.

examples.pinyin: Zhè bǎ dāo shì tiě zuò de.
examples.en: This knife is made of iron.

examples.pinyin: Tiělù yùnshū hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: Railway transportation is very important.

停止.etymology: This compound word combines two characters both meaning 'to stop': 停 (to halt/park) and 止 (to cease). The redundancy emphasizes the meaning of complete cessation. Both characters retain their traditional meanings in the simplified system.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng tíngzhǐ shuōhuà.
examples.en: Please stop talking.

examples.pinyin: Yǔ zhōngyú tíngzhǐ le.
examples.en: The rain finally stopped.

挺.etymology: Originally meant to straighten or stand upright, using the hand radical 扌 and 廷 for phonetic value. The meaning evolved to 'quite' or 'rather' as an intensifier, from the sense of standing out or being prominent.

examples.pinyin: Zhège diànyǐng tǐng hǎokàn de.
examples.en: This movie is quite good.

examples.pinyin: Jīntiān tiānqì tǐng lěng de.
examples.en: The weather is rather cold today.

components.note: refers to work or business activities

同事.etymology: This compound word combines 同 (same/together) with 事 (work/affairs) to describe people who work together or share the same work responsibilities. The term literally means 'same work' and refers to professional relationships in a workplace.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì wǒ de tóngshì.
examples.en: He is my colleague.

examples.zh: 我和同事们一起吃午饭。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hé tóngshìmen yīqǐ chī wǔfān.
examples.en: I have lunch together with my colleagues.

痛苦.etymology: This compound word combines 痛 (pain) with 苦 (bitter/suffering) to intensify the meaning of distress or anguish. Both characters relate to unpleasant experiences, creating a strong expression for physical or emotional suffering.

examples.pinyin: Tā hěn tòngkǔ.
examples.en: He is very painful/suffering.

examples.zh: 失去亲人让她感到非常痛苦。
examples.pinyin: Shīqù qīnrén ràng tā gǎndào fēicháng tòngkǔ.
examples.en: Losing a loved one made her feel extremely painful.

偷.etymology: This character consists of the person radical 亻 indicating human action, combined with 俞 which serves as a phonetic component. The character originally depicted the action of a person taking something secretly or without permission.

examples.pinyin: Bùyào tōu dōngxi.
examples.en: Don't steal things.

examples.pinyin: Xiǎotōu tōuzǒule tā de qiánbāo.
examples.en: The thief stole her wallet.

components.note: refers to the material substance of ground

土地.etymology: This compound word combines 土 (earth/soil) with 地 (ground/land) to refer to land as both a physical substance and a geographical area. The combination emphasizes both the material and spatial aspects of land or territory.

examples.pinyin: Zhè kuài tǔdì hěn féiwò.
examples.en: This piece of land is very fertile.

examples.zh: 农民在土地上种植粮食。
examples.pinyin: Nóngmín zài tǔdì shàng zhòngzhí liángshi.
examples.en: Farmers grow crops on the land.

吐.etymology: This character consists of the mouth radical 口 indicating oral action, combined with 土 which serves as a phonetic component. The character represents the action of expelling something from the mouth, whether spitting or vomiting.

examples.pinyin: Tā tùle yī kǒu tán.
examples.en: He spat out some phlegm.

examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào ěxin, xiǎng yào tù.
examples.en: She felt nauseous and wanted to vomit.

components.note: provides phonetic element in the simplified form

团.etymology: The traditional form 團 was much more complex with multiple components. The simplified form 团 uses the enclosure radical 囗 with 才 inside, creating a much simpler structure while maintaining the core meaning of something unified or grouped together.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen shì yīgè tuánduì.
examples.en: We are a team.

examples.zh: 他加入了学校的篮球团。
examples.pinyin: Tā jiārù le xuéxiào de lánqiú tuán.
examples.en: He joined the school basketball team.

components.note: metaphorically pushing something away in time

推迟.etymology: This compound word combines 推 (to push) and 迟 (late/slow) to create the meaning of pushing something to a later time. The metaphor of physically pushing something away extends to pushing events forward in time.

examples.pinyin: Huìyì tuīchí dào míngtiān.
examples.en: The meeting is postponed until tomorrow.

examples.zh: 由于天气不好，比赛被推迟了。
examples.pinyin: Yóuyú tiānqì bù hǎo, bǐsài bèi tuīchí le.
examples.en: Due to bad weather, the competition was postponed.

挖.etymology: This character combines the hand radical 扌 with the phonetic component 圭. The hand radical clearly indicates the manual action of digging, while 圭 provides the pronunciation.

examples.pinyin: Xiǎo gǒu zài huāyuán lǐ wā dòng.
examples.en: The puppy is digging holes in the garden.

examples.zh: 工人们正在挖一条新的地下管道。
examples.pinyin: Gōngrén men zhèngzài wā yītiáo xīn de dìxià guǎndào.
examples.en: The workers are digging a new underground pipeline.

components.note: indicates curved shape like a bow

弯.etymology: The traditional form 彎 was much more complex. The simplified form 弯 uses the bow radical 弓 to represent the curved shape, combined with a simplified phonetic component. A bow naturally represents the concept of something curved or bent.

examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù hěn wān.
examples.en: This road is very curved.

examples.pinyin: Tā wān xià yāo jiǎn qǐ le qiánbāo.
examples.en: He bent down and picked up the wallet.

components.note: contributes the sense of goodness or excellence

完善.etymology: This compound word combines 完 (complete) and 善 (good), creating the meaning of something being both complete and good, hence 'perfect'. The word emphasizes the dual aspects of completeness and excellence. It can function both as an adjective meaning 'perfect' and as a verb meaning 'to perfect' or 'to improve'.

examples.pinyin: Zhège jìhuà hěn wánshàn.
examples.en: This plan is very perfect.

examples.zh: 我们需要完善这个系统。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào wánshàn zhège xìtǒng.
examples.en: We need to perfect this system.

components.note: indicates a physical object or implement

玩具.etymology: This compound combines 玩 (to play) with 具 (tool/implement), literally meaning 'play tool' or 'playing implement'. The word structure reflects the functional nature of toys as objects specifically designed for play and entertainment. Both characters have ancient origins, with 玩 originally depicting hands handling jade, and 具 representing various tools or utensils.

examples.pinyin: Háizimen xǐhuān wánjù.
examples.en: Children like toys.

examples.zh: 这个玩具店有很多种类的玩具。
examples.pinyin: Zhège wánjùdiàn yǒu hěn duō zhǒnglèi de wánjù.
examples.en: This toy store has many types of toys.

components.note: provides the metaphor of interconnected structure
emphasizes the connecting or linking function

网络.etymology: This modern compound combines 网 (net/web) with 络 (to connect), creating a metaphor for interconnected systems. Originally used for any kind of network system, it became the standard term for computer networks and the internet. The combination effectively captures the essence of networks as web-like structures that connect various points or nodes together.

examples.pinyin: Wǎngluò hěn zhòngyào.
examples.en: The network is very important.

examples.zh: 现在的网络技术发展很快。
examples.pinyin: Xiànzài de wǎngluò jìshù fāzhǎn hěn kuài.
examples.en: Current network technology is developing very quickly.

components.note: reinforces and intensifies the meaning of danger

危险.etymology: This compound combines two characters both relating to danger: 危 (danger/peril) and 险 (dangerous/risky). Both characters have ancient origins, with 危 originally depicting a person on a high cliff, and 险 combining mountain radicals with the idea of difficulty or obstruction. The combination creates a stronger emphasis on the concept of danger than either character alone.

examples.pinyin: Zhèlǐ hěn wēixiǎn.
examples.en: It's very dangerous here.

examples.zh: 开车时打电话是危险的行为。
examples.pinyin: Kāichē shí dǎ diànhuà shì wēixiǎn de xíngwéi.
examples.en: Making phone calls while driving is dangerous behavior.

components.note: indicates the gentle, subtle nature of the expression
main semantic component indicating the facial expression

微笑.etymology: This compound combines 微 (slight/subtle) with 笑 (laugh/smile), specifically describing a gentle, subtle smile as opposed to loud laughter. The word distinguishes a soft, gentle smile from more overt expressions of joy or amusement. 微 adds a nuance of gentleness and subtlety to the basic concept of 笑, creating a more refined and tender expression.

examples.pinyin: Tā duì wǒ wēixiào.
examples.en: She smiled at me.

examples.zh: 老师总是带着温暖的微笑面对学生。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī zǒng shì dàizhe wēnnuǎn de wēixiào miànduì xuéshēng.
examples.en: The teacher always faces students with a warm smile.

围.etymology: The character consists of the 囗 (enclosure) radical surrounding 韦, which is the simplified form of 韋 (leather). The traditional form 圍 used 韋 (leather) as the phonetic component. In simplification, 韋 was reduced to 韦. The enclosure radical 囗 provides the semantic meaning of surrounding or enclosing, while the inner component provides the pronunciation.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen wéizhe zhuōzi zuò.
examples.en: We sit around the table.

examples.zh: 警察围住了那栋建筑物。
examples.pinyin: Jǐngchá wéizhù le nà dòng jiànzhùwù.
examples.en: The police surrounded that building.

components.note: describes the scarf's function of wrapping around
indicates the material nature of the item

围巾.etymology: A compound word combining 围 (to surround) and 巾 (cloth/towel). The term literally describes a piece of cloth that surrounds or wraps around the neck. This straightforward descriptive compound reflects the practical function of the garment.

examples.pinyin: Tiān lěng le, wǒ dài wéijīn.
examples.en: It's cold, so I'm wearing a scarf.

examples.zh: 这条红色的围巾很漂亮。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo hóngsè de wéijīn hěn piàoliang.
examples.en: This red scarf is very beautiful.

components.note: first character meaning warm or mild
second character meaning degree or measure

温度.etymology: This compound combines 温 (warm, mild) and 度 (degree, measure) to create the scientific term for temperature. The word literally means 'degree of warmth' and has been used in modern Chinese to translate the scientific concept of temperature.

examples.pinyin: jīn tiān de wēn dù hěn gāo.
examples.en: Today's temperature is very high.

examples.zh: 医生用体温计测量病人的体温度数。
examples.pinyin: yī shēng yòng tǐ wēn jì cè liáng bìng rén de tǐ wēn dù shù.
examples.en: The doctor uses a thermometer to measure the patient's body temperature.

握手.etymology: A compound word combining 握 (to grasp, hold) with 手 (hand). This term literally describes the physical action of grasping hands, which became the standard greeting gesture. The combination directly represents the physical mechanics of the handshake gesture.

examples.pinyin: Jiànmiàn shí wǒmen wòshǒu le.
examples.en: We shook hands when we met.

examples.zh: 在商务场合，握手是一种礼貌的问候方式。
examples.pinyin: Zài shāngwù chǎnghé, wòshǒu shì yī zhǒng lǐmào de wènhòu fāngshì.
examples.en: In business settings, shaking hands is a polite way of greeting.

components.note: first character meaning mistake or to err
second character meaning to understand or comprehend

误会.etymology: This compound combines 误 (mistake, error) and 会 (to understand, comprehend) to express misunderstanding. The term literally means 'mistaken understanding.' The simplified forms use 误 instead of traditional 誤 and 会 instead of traditional 會.

examples.pinyin: zhè zhǐ shì gè wù huì.
examples.en: This is just a misunderstanding.

examples.zh: 因为语言不通，他们之间经常发生误会。
examples.pinyin: yīn wèi yǔ yán bù tōng, tā men zhī jiān jīng cháng fā shēng wù huì.
examples.en: Because of language barriers, misunderstandings often occur between them.

components.note: second character describing the red color
third character referring to persimmon fruit

西红柿.etymology: This compound literally means 'western red persimmon.' The name reflects how tomatoes were perceived when first introduced to China - as red fruits from the west that resembled persimmons. The simplified form uses 红 instead of traditional 紅, while 西 and 柿 remain unchanged.

examples.pinyin: wǒ xǐ huan chī xī hóng shì.
examples.en: I like to eat tomatoes.

examples.zh: 这道西红柿鸡蛋面味道很好，营养也很丰富。
examples.pinyin: zhè dào xī hóng shì jī dàn miàn wèi dào hěn hǎo, yíng yǎng yě hěn fēng fù.
examples.en: This tomato and egg noodle dish tastes great and is also very nutritious.

吸引.etymology: A compound verb combining 吸 (to absorb, suck in) and 引 (to lead, draw). The two characters work together to express the concept of drawing something toward oneself. This compound reflects the Chinese tendency to combine related verbs to create more specific meanings.

examples.zh: 这个地方吸引了很多游客。
examples.pinyin: Zhège dìfang xīyǐnle hěn duō yóukè.
examples.en: This place attracts many tourists.

examples.zh: 她被这本书的内容深深吸引。
examples.pinyin: Tā bèi zhè běn shū de nèiróng shēnshēn xīyǐn.
examples.en: She was deeply attracted by the content of this book.

系.etymology: Originally depicted silk threads being tied together, representing connection and binding. The character evolved to mean systems of organization, academic departments, and the act of tying or connecting things together.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì zhōngwén xì de xuésheng.
examples.en: He is a student in the Chinese department.

examples.pinyin: Zhège wèntí xì guān zhòngyào.
examples.en: This issue is of vital importance.

咸.etymology: Originally composed of 口 (mouth) indicating taste and 戈 (halberd) providing the phonetic element. The character represents the salty taste sensation experienced in the mouth. This is one of the fundamental taste characters in Chinese, alongside sweet, sour, bitter, and spicy.

examples.pinyin: Zhège cài tài xiánle.
examples.en: This dish is too salty.

examples.zh: 医生建议他少吃咸的食物。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiànyì tā shǎo chī xián de shíwù.
examples.en: The doctor advised him to eat less salty food.

相反.etymology: A compound word combining 相 (mutual, reciprocal) and 反 (opposite, reverse). The term describes a mutual state of opposition or contradiction between two things. The 相 emphasizes the relational aspect while 反 provides the core meaning of opposition.

examples.pinyin: Tā de xiǎngfǎ hé wǒ xiāngfǎn.
examples.en: His ideas are opposite to mine.

examples.zh: 相反，我觉得这样做会更好。
examples.pinyin: Xiāngfǎn, wǒ juéde zhèyàng zuò huì gèng hǎo.
examples.en: On the contrary, I think doing it this way would be better.

components.note: refers to a person or companion
suffix indicating a person, especially male

小伙子.etymology: A colloquial term literally meaning 'small fellow person'. The word combines 小 (small/young) with 伙子 (fellow/guy), creating an informal way to refer to a young man. It carries a friendly, approachable tone in Chinese speech.

examples.pinyin: Nàge xiǎo huǒzi hěn shuài.
examples.en: That young man is very handsome.

examples.zh: 这个小伙子工作很努力。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiǎo huǒzi gōngzuò hěn nǔlì.
examples.en: This young man works very hard.

components.note: indicates a lesser or informal type of writing

小说.etymology: Literally means 'small talk' or 'minor storytelling'. The term distinguishes fictional narrative literature from formal historical or philosophical writing. It emerged to describe imaginative prose works as opposed to serious scholarly texts.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān kàn xiǎoshuō.
examples.en: I like reading novels.

examples.zh: 这是一本很有名的历史小说。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī běn hěn yǒumíng de lìshǐ xiǎoshuō.
examples.en: This is a very famous historical novel.

信封.etymology: A compound word combining 信 (letter) and 封 (seal/envelope). Originally 封 referred to sealing documents, and later extended to mean the envelope itself that contains letters.

examples.pinyin: qǐng zài xìn fēng shàng xiě dì zhǐ
examples.en: Please write the address on the envelope.

examples.zh: 他收到了一个厚厚的信封。
examples.pinyin: tā shōu dào le yí gè hòu hòu de xìn fēng
examples.en: He received a thick envelope.

行.etymology: The character depicts the act of walking, showing alternating steps with left and right feet. Originally representing physical movement, it extended to mean 'to go,' 'to do,' and eventually 'okay' or 'acceptable' in modern usage.

examples.pinyin: Xíng, wǒ tóng yì.
examples.en: Okay, I agree.

examples.pinyin: Zhè yàng zuò xíng bù xíng?
examples.en: Is it okay to do it this way?

形状.etymology: This compound combines 形 (form, shape) and 状 (shape, condition) to specifically refer to the physical shape or form of objects. Both characters relate to appearance and form, reinforcing the meaning.

examples.zh: 这个苹果的形状很奇怪。
examples.pinyin: Zhège píngguǒ de xíngzhuàng hěn qíguài.
examples.en: This apple has a strange shape.

examples.zh: 建筑师设计了一个独特形状的建筑物。
examples.pinyin: Jiànzhùshī shèjìle yīgè dútè xíngzhuàng de jiànzhùwù.
examples.en: The architect designed a building with a unique shape.

性别.etymology: A compound word combining 性 (nature/sex) and 别 (distinguish/separate). It refers to the biological or social categorization that distinguishes male from female.

examples.pinyin: qǐng tián xiě xìng bié
examples.en: Please fill in your gender.

examples.zh: 性别不应该影响工作机会。
examples.pinyin: xìng bié bù yīng gāi yǐng xiǎng gōng zuò jī huì
examples.en: Gender should not affect job opportunities.

血.etymology: An ancient pictographic character depicting blood. The simplified form retains the basic structure of the traditional character. The character shows a vessel or container with liquid inside, representing the concept of blood as a vital bodily fluid.

examples.pinyin: Tā liú le hěn duō xuè.
examples.en: He bled a lot.

examples.zh: 医生需要检查病人的血液成分。
examples.pinyin: Yī shēng xū yào jiǎn chá bìng rén de xuè yè chéng fèn.
examples.en: The doctor needs to examine the patient's blood composition.

亚洲.etymology: A geographical term combining 亚 (phonetic element approximating 'Asia') and 洲 (continent). The character 亚 is a simplified form of 亞, used here purely for its phonetic value rather than its original meaning. This represents a phonetic borrowing to render the foreign geographical name 'Asia' into Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Zhōng guó zài yà zhōu.
examples.en: China is in Asia.

examples.zh: 亚洲是世界上最大的洲。
examples.pinyin: Yà zhōu shì shì jiè shàng zuì dà de zhōu.
examples.en: Asia is the largest continent in the world.

components.note: bottom component, simplified from traditional salt radical

盐.etymology: The traditional form 鹽 contained the salt radical 鹵. In simplification, this was replaced with the simpler 卤 component. The top portion 臣 may relate to the administrative importance of salt in ancient times.

examples.pinyin: zhè cài tài xián le, yán fàng duō le.
examples.en: This dish is too salty, too much salt was added.

examples.pinyin: yīshēng jiànyì tā shǎo chī yán.
examples.en: The doctor advised him to eat less salt.

components.note: second character meaning light or radiance

阳光.etymology: Compound word combining 阳 (sun, yang energy) and 光 (light). Together they refer to the light and warmth from the sun, creating the meaning of sunshine or sunlight.

examples.pinyin: jīntiān yángguāng hěn hǎo.
examples.en: The sunshine is very nice today.

examples.zh: 阳光透过窗户照进房间。
examples.pinyin: yángguāng tòuguò chuānghu zhào jìn fángjiān.
examples.en: Sunlight shines into the room through the window.

components.note: first character meaning type or manner
second character used as nominal suffix

样子.etymology: Compound word where 样 means 'kind' or 'type' and 子 serves as a nominal suffix. Together they refer to the way something looks or appears, describing physical appearance or manner.

examples.pinyin: nǐ kàn tā de yàngzi hěn lèi.
examples.en: Look at him, he appears very tired.

examples.pinyin: zhè fángzi de yàngzi hěn tèbié.
examples.en: The appearance of this house is very special.

components.note: 十 (ten) + 目 (eye) + 一 (one) indicating directness

一直.etymology: Combines 一 (one/continuous) with 直 (straight/direct) to express continuous, uninterrupted action or state. The meaning extends from physical straightness to temporal continuity - doing something continuously without deviation.

examples.pinyin: Tā yī zhí zài xué xí.
examples.en: He has been studying all along.

examples.zh: 我们一直沿着这条路走。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ men yī zhí yán zhe zhè tiáo lù zǒu.
examples.en: We kept walking straight along this road.

以.etymology: Ancient character showing a person using or taking something. Originally depicted the concept of 'using' or 'by means of'. The character has maintained its basic form and core meaning of instrumentality throughout Chinese history.

examples.pinyin: Yǐ hòu wǒ huì gèng xiǎo xīn.
examples.en: I will be more careful in the future.

examples.pinyin: Tā yǐ yóu xì wéi shēng.
examples.en: He makes a living by gaming.

以后.etymology: This compound combines 以 (with, by) and 后 (after, behind). Together they form a temporal expression meaning 'after' or 'in the future', indicating time that comes later than a reference point.

examples.pinyin: Yǐhòu wǒ huì nǔlì xuéxí.
examples.en: I will study hard in the future.

examples.zh: 从今以后不要再犯同样的错误。
examples.pinyin: Cóng jīn yǐhòu bùyào zài fàn tóngyàng de cuòwù.
examples.en: Don't make the same mistake again from now on.

components.note: second character meaning joy or music

音乐.etymology: This compound word combines 音 (sound) and 乐 (joy/music). The second character 乐 is simplified from traditional 樂. Together they literally mean 'sound-joy', referring to the art of organized sound that brings pleasure.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐ huān tīng yīn yuè.
examples.en: I like listening to music.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shǒu yīn yuè hěn yōu měi.
examples.en: This piece of music is very beautiful.

components.note: first character meaning 'silver', referring to money/currency
second character meaning 'business' or 'firm' in this context

银行.etymology: This compound word combines 银 (silver) and 行 (business/firm) to mean 'bank'. The term originated from the historical association of banks with silver currency and precious metal trading. The character 行 here refers to a commercial establishment or business house.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yào qù yín háng qǔ qián.
examples.en: I need to go to the bank to withdraw money.

examples.pinyin: zhè jiā yín háng de fú wù hěn hǎo.
examples.en: This bank's service is very good.

components.note: first character meaning position or seat
second character meaning to place or arrange

位置.etymology: This compound combines 位 (position/place) and 置 (to place/put). Both characters relate to spatial arrangement and positioning, together forming a noun that means location or position in space.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ de wèi zhì zài qián miàn.
examples.en: My position is in the front.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng gào sù wǒ yín háng de wèi zhì.
examples.en: Please tell me the location of the bank.

components.note: first character meaning dirty or to soil
second character meaning to dye or contaminate

污染.etymology: This compound combines 污 (dirty, to soil) and 染 (to dye, to contaminate). Together they express the concept of environmental contamination or pollution. This modern term developed as awareness of environmental issues grew in the 20th century.

examples.pinyin: kōng qì wū rǎn hěn yán zhòng.
examples.en: Air pollution is very serious.

examples.zh: 工厂排放的废水污染了这条河流。
examples.pinyin: gōng chǎng pái fàng de fèi shuǐ wū rǎn le zhè tiáo hé liú.
examples.en: The wastewater discharged by the factory has polluted this river.

components.note: first character meaning military or martial
second character meaning skill or technique

武术.etymology: This compound combines 武 (martial, military) and 术 (skill, technique) to mean martial arts or combat techniques. The term encompasses various traditional Chinese fighting styles and combat methods. The simplified form uses 术 instead of the traditional 術.

examples.pinyin: tā cóng xiǎo jiù xué wǔ shù.
examples.en: He has been learning martial arts since childhood.

examples.zh: 中国武术有着悠久的历史和丰富的文化内涵。
examples.pinyin: zhōng guó wǔ shù yǒu zhe yōu jiǔ de lì shǐ hé fēng fù de wén huà nèi hán.
examples.en: Chinese martial arts have a long history and rich cultural connotations.

洗衣机.etymology: A compound noun literally meaning 'wash-clothes-machine'. This follows the typical Chinese pattern for naming appliances by combining the action (洗), the object (衣), and the device type (机). The structure clearly describes the function of the machine.

examples.pinyin: Xǐyījī huàile.
examples.en: The washing machine is broken.

examples.zh: 我需要买一台新的洗衣机。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xūyào mǎi yī tái xīn de xǐyījī.
examples.en: I need to buy a new washing machine.

components.note: indicates a specific entry or category
reinforces the meaning of categorized items

项目.etymology: This compound combines 项 (item, clause) with 目 (eye, item). The word originally referred to items in a list or catalogue, later extending to mean projects. The simplified 项 replaced traditional 項, removing the 頁 component.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè xiàng mù hěn zhòng yào
examples.en: This project is very important.

examples.zh: 公司正在开展几个新的研发项目。
examples.pinyin: gōng sī zhèng zài kāi zhǎn jǐ gè xīn de yán fā xiàng mù
examples.en: The company is developing several new research projects.

components.note: reinforces the concept of action or behavior

行为.etymology: A compound word combining 行 (action/conduct) and 为 (act/do). Both characters relate to actions and doing, creating emphasis on behavioral patterns and conduct.

examples.pinyin: tā de xíng wéi hěn qí guài
examples.en: His behavior is strange.

examples.zh: 我们要为自己的行为负责。
examples.pinyin: wǒ men yào wèi zì jǐ de xíng wéi fù zé
examples.en: We must be responsible for our own behavior.

components.note: indicates the body part being cleaned

牙膏.etymology: This compound combines 牙 (tooth, teeth) with 膏 (paste, ointment). Together they literally mean 'tooth paste', referring to the paste-like substance used for cleaning teeth. The term directly describes both the target (teeth) and the form (paste) of this dental hygiene product.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yòng wán yágāo le.
examples.en: I've used up the toothpaste.

examples.zh: 选择含氟的牙膏对牙齿更好。
examples.pinyin: Xuǎnzé hánfú de yágāo duì yáchǐ gèng hǎo.
examples.en: Choosing fluoride toothpaste is better for teeth.

components.note: simplified from 鏡, refers to optical device

眼镜.etymology: A compound word meaning 'eye mirrors' or lenses for eyes. 眼 depicts an eye with the eye radical 目. 镜 is simplified from traditional 鏡, combining metal radical 金 with 竟 (complete), originally meaning polished metal mirror.

examples.pinyin: wǒ dài yǎn jìng.
examples.en: I wear glasses.

examples.pinyin: tā de yǎn jìng huài le.
examples.en: His glasses are broken.

爷爷.etymology: A reduplication of 爷 (grandfather/father) to create an affectionate, familiar term for grandfather. The repetition pattern is common in Chinese kinship terms to express intimacy and endearment, similar to 妈妈, 爸爸.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yé ye jīn nián bā shí suì.
examples.en: My grandfather is eighty years old this year.

examples.pinyin: Yé ye jīng cháng gěi wǒ jiǎng gù shi.
examples.en: Grandfather often tells me stories.

系统.etymology: Compound word combining 系 (connection, tie) and 统 (unity, control). Together they represent an organized structure where components are systematically connected and unified under coherent principles or governance.

examples.pinyin: Diànnǎo xìtǒng chū wèntí le.
examples.en: The computer system has a problem.

examples.zh: 我们需要建立完善的管理系统。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào jiànlì wánshàn de guǎnlǐ xìtǒng.
examples.en: We need to establish a comprehensive management system.

细节.etymology: Compound word combining 细 (fine, minute) and 节 (section, part). Together they refer to the small, specific parts or aspects of something that require careful attention and examination.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng zhùyì xìjié.
examples.en: Please pay attention to the details.

examples.zh: 成功往往取决于细节的处理。
examples.pinyin: Chénggōng wǎngwǎng qǔjué yú xìjié de chǔlǐ.
examples.en: Success often depends on handling the details.

components.note: radical indicating speech or vocal action

吓.etymology: Phonosemantic compound with 口 (mouth) as semantic component indicating vocal expression, and 下 (down) as phonetic component. The character represents the act of frightening someone through vocal means or causing fear.

examples.pinyin: Bié xià wǒ!
examples.en: Don't scare me!

examples.zh: 这个电影把孩子们吓哭了。
examples.pinyin: Zhège diànyǐng bǎ háizimen xià kū le.
examples.en: This movie scared the children to tears.

components.note: indicates temporary residence or organized group

夏令营.etymology: Three-character compound meaning 'summer camp.' 夏 refers to the summer season, 令 indicates organized commands or activities, and 营 refers to a camp or organized group living arrangement. Together they describe organized recreational or educational camps held during summer.

examples.pinyin: Háizimen qù xiàlìngyíng le.
examples.en: The children went to summer camp.

examples.zh: 这个夏令营有很多有趣的活动。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiàlìngyíng yǒu hěn duō yǒuqù de huódòng.
examples.en: This summer camp has many interesting activities.

components.note: grammatical particle indicating result or degree

显得.etymology: Compound verb combining 显 (to show, appear) with 得 (resultative particle). Together they form a verb meaning 'to appear as' or 'to seem,' indicating how something manifests or presents itself to others.

examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎnde hěn lèi.
examples.en: She seems very tired.

examples.zh: 这件衣服让她显得更年轻。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu ràng tā xiǎnde gèng niánqīng.
examples.en: This clothing makes her appear younger.

显然.etymology: Compound adverb combining 显 (clear, manifest) and 然 (naturally, so). Together they mean 'obviously' or 'clearly,' indicating that something is manifestly apparent or naturally evident without need for explanation.

examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎnrán bù gāoxìng.
examples.en: He is obviously unhappy.

examples.zh: 显然，这个计划需要更多时间。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎnrán, zhège jìhuà xūyào gèng duō shíjiān.
examples.en: Obviously, this plan needs more time.

显示.etymology: Compound verb combining 显 (to show, manifest) and 示 (to show, indicate). Both characters relate to making something visible or apparent, creating an emphatic verb meaning 'to display' or 'to demonstrate clearly.'

examples.pinyin: Diànnǎo xiǎnshì cuòwù xìnxī.
examples.en: The computer displays an error message.

examples.zh: 数据显示情况正在好转。
examples.pinyin: Shùjù xiǎnshì qíngkuàng zhèngzài hǎozhuǎn.
examples.en: The data shows that the situation is improving.

现实.etymology: A compound word combining 现 (present/appear) and 实 (real/solid) to express the concept of 'what actually exists' or 'reality'. This combination emphasizes both the manifest and substantial nature of actual conditions.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào miànduì xiànshí.
examples.en: We need to face reality.

examples.pinyin: Tā de xiǎngfǎ bù tài xiànshí.
examples.en: His ideas are not very realistic.

现象.etymology: A compound word combining 现 (appear/manifest) and 象 (image/appearance) to describe observable occurrences or manifestations. This term translates the philosophical concept of 'phenomenon' as something that appears to our senses.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yí ge yǒuqù de xiànxiàng.
examples.en: This is an interesting phenomenon.

examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiā yánjiū zìrán xiànxiàng.
examples.en: Scientists study natural phenomena.

限制.etymology: A compound word combining 限 (limit/boundary) and 制 (control/regulate) to express the concept of setting boundaries or controlling within certain parameters. The combination emphasizes both the boundary aspect and the regulatory control.

examples.pinyin: Zhèlǐ xiànzhì tíngchē.
examples.en: Parking is restricted here.

examples.pinyin: Zhèngfǔ xiànzhìle fángjià shàngzhǎng.
examples.en: The government has limited the rise in housing prices.

相当.etymology: A compound word combining 相 (mutual/reciprocal) and 当 (match/equal) to express equivalence or a considerable degree. Originally meaning 'mutually matching' or 'equivalent', it evolved to also mean 'quite' or 'rather' as an adverb.

examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū xiāngdāng yǒuqù.
examples.en: This book is quite interesting.

examples.zh: 他的中文水平相当于三年级学生。
examples.pinyin: Tā de Zhōngwén shuǐpíng xiāngdāng yú sān niánjí xuésheng.
examples.en: His Chinese level is equivalent to that of a third-year student.

相对.etymology: A compound word combining 相 (mutual/reciprocal) and 对 (opposite/face) to express the concept of things being in relation to each other, either as opposites or as relative positions. Used both for 'relative' (as opposed to absolute) and 'opposite' meanings.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì xiāngduì jiǎndān de wèntí.
examples.en: This is a relatively simple problem.

examples.pinyin: Liǎng zhǒng guāndiǎn xiāng duìlì.
examples.en: The two viewpoints are opposite to each other.

相关.etymology: A compound word combining 相 (mutual/reciprocal) and 关 (connection/concern) to express mutual connection or relevance between things. The word emphasizes the interconnected nature of related items or concepts.

examples.pinyin: Zhè liǎng jiàn shì xiāngguān.
examples.en: These two things are related.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng tígōng xiāngguān wénjiàn.
examples.en: Please provide the relevant documents.

相似.etymology: This compound word combines 相 (mutual, each other) with 似 (to resemble). The meaning develops from the idea of mutually resembling each other. Both characters retain their original forms in simplified Chinese.

examples.pinyin: zhè liǎng gè cí hěn xiāng sì
examples.en: These two words are very similar.

examples.zh: 他们的想法相似，都支持这个计划。
examples.pinyin: tā men de xiǎng fǎ xiāng sì, dōu zhī chí zhè gè jì huà
examples.en: Their ideas are similar; they both support this plan.

components.note: indicates a mark or indication of something
